step.title: Discovery & Context
step.goal: Understand what agent the user wants to create, determine its mode (primary vs subagent), and identify the skills it needs.

substep.title: Gather Requirements
substep.ask: What agent would you like to create? Please describe: - What is its primary purpose? - Who will use it? (users directly, or spawned by other agents?) - Is there an architecture document or spec I should reference?

check.action: Identify integration points with other agents

Conduct lightweight discovery: - Core responsibilities (what problems does it solve?) - Interaction model (primary or subagent?) - Tool needs (file access, web search, bash, MCP tools?) - Skill needs (what workflows or processes?)

substep.title: Determine Mode
substep.action:
Determine whether this is a primary agent or subagent:
Primary (user-facing): - Appears in agent picker, has menu and persona - Users interact with it directly - Can spawn subagents (orchestrator pattern)
Subagent (specialist): - Spawned by other agents, stateless between invocations - Focused on single responsibility, returns structured results - No user interaction, no menu

substep.title: Identify Skills
substep.action: Identify the skills this agent needs. For each: 1. Skill name (kebab-case) 2. Brief description 3. Whether it needs its own workflow steps, templates, or scripts
substep.note:
An agent's value comes from its skills. 3-6 is the sweet spot. Too few (0-2): question if this needs to be an agent. Too many (7+): consider splitting into multiple agents.
For subagents: at least one skill is REQUIRED. Agent instructions define identity. Skills define capability (methodology).

substep.title: Confirm Understanding
substep.template-output:
Summarize: - Agent purpose - Mode: primary / subagent - Target users or calling agents - Skills identified (name + type + purpose table) - Key constraints or requirements
Present to user for confirmation.
[a] Explore more [c] Continue [y] YOLO

section.title: Orchestrator + Specialist Pattern
section.description: When an agent does context-heavy operations (web searches, large codebase scans, exhaustive file reading), consider splitting into an orchestrator that delegates to specialist subagents.

signals.title: When to recommend subagents
signals.signal: Agent does extensive web searching (search results burn context)
Agent scans large codebases (file listings fill context)
Different operations need different permission sets

section.title: Good Discovery Questions
section.example: "What's the primary problem this agent solves?" "Who will interact with this agent directly?" "What should this agent NEVER do?" "Does this agent need to delegate heavy work to specialists?"
